title: Hiring managers prefer goods-upfront approach over relationship-building
summary: Alex (former hiring manager) validated hypothesis that hiring managers prefer seeing candidates immediately vs. establishing relationship first. Direct quote: 'I don't want to start a conversation with every recruiter. I want to see candidates.'

title: Subscription model contingent on placement proof
summary: Companies express subscription interest BUT require 'show me at least one good candidate first.' Must use warm intro pricing initially, transition to subscription after trust established through successful placements.

title: PM hiring more chaotic than engineering hiring
summary: PM roles have 'insanely varied backgrounds' making hiring chaotic. Unlike engineering (15yr experience = obviously skilled), PMs can be career transitions, IC contributors, varied paths. Perfect match for Careerspan's strength in surfacing atypical profiles.

title: Break the mold all the way - question all recruiting priors
summary: If disrupting, disrupt completely. Don't just filter better - present different format (cards not docs), different info (stories not bullets), different channel (hiring managers not HR), different approach (goods first not relationship).

title: Alex Caveny - Now available for talent referrals
summary: Alex evolved from pure advisor to potential talent referrer. Knows engineers/PMs, willing to refer. Also potential customer if advising companies with hiring needs. Next session: October.

title: HR resists disruption due to job security
summary: Alex insight: 'HR likes their normal process - it's their job security.' Hiring managers are burned out and want solutions; HR sees disruption as competition. Target hiring managers directly.
